entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 23 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 23 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-23 11:30AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • AFL 2022 round 19 LIVE updates: Dogs closing in on Demons, Suns on top of Lions
  • Cats survive Port threat but lose Stanley to ‘medium term’ knee injury
  • Liberals choose councillor with controversial trans views to replace Bernie Finn
  • Tasmania threatens to withdraw ‘unprecedented’ expansion funding offer if AFL plays hardball
  • Tempers flare as patient Penrith put away spirited Sharks
  • Collingwood mourning the death of club great Billy Picken
  • Small change: Pay rises fail to match soaring inflation
  • Kirkstall triple death: Men ‘ambushed’ on country road, victim’s family says
  • Crowdfunding reprieve for Earlwood pensioners facing strata bankruptcy
  • Champagne Charlie bubbles up in Melbourne, a town that enjoys a glass of fizz
  • Genetic banks and cleaning mats: Preparing for the arrival of foot and mouth
  • Federal government insists foot and mouth controls on track, despite relaxed measures at airports
  • Best of cartoons, July 23, 2022
  • The latest illustrations from artist Alan Moir
  • Greens to flex Senate muscle in push for sick-pay safety net
  • Give councils cash to help bring down housing prices: think tank
  • Australia is the extinction superpower of the world. Here’s what we need to do
  • Union boss says working from home will harm economy, vaccinations
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Davidson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Michael Leunig
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • Holidaymakers turned around after ski resorts hit capacity
  • Winter deluge cancels local sport, climate change threatens to ruin it
  • Splendour on the street: party lives on in town
  • Sydney kickboxer extradited from Thailand charged with alleged Leichhardt murder
  • Rain to ease before returning in Sydney, as Splendour given green light
  • Storm clouds gathering over Perrottet’s Asian trip
  • ‘Not a single person will talk to you’: Why it’s so hard to make friends in Sydney
  • On the rise: Owners of Reddam House to turn Harry Seidler building into new school
  • Urgent shift needed to embrace renewables
  • ‘Beacon of hope’: Ukraine, Russia sign UN deal to export grain
  • Mossad probe finds Buenos Aires bombings had no local help from Iran
  • It’s so hot in China, melting glaciers risk collapsing dams
  • US considers monkeypox as a health emergency after first child cases
  • James Webb telescope spots the oldest galaxy back in time
  • Prince Harry wins right to challenge decision on police protection
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Trump’s ex-adviser Steve Bannon convicted of contempt of US Congress
  • ‘We need Australians more than ever’: Inside Bali’s battle against foot and mouth
  • Three people charged over the murder of journalist and indigenous expert in Amazon
  • Jos’s dying father pleaded for mercy 42 years ago. It haunts her to this day
  • On the trail of a killer: Police test crucial evidence 40 years on
  • Our mosque was attacked, but our faith keeps the doors open to all
  • E-gate: The inner Melbourne suburb that could have been
  • Qld hospitals jam packed with record number of COVID patients
  • Bali travellers breeze through Melbourne Airport despite promised crackdown on foot and mouth
  • Landing your first job: tips and tricks for starters
  • What does the ANZ-Suncorp deal say about banking competition in Australia?
  • Teaching in remote communities, ‘you can make a real difference’
  • Is group chat, in reality, pointless chit-chat?
  • Are universities neglecting the entrepreneurs of the future?
  • Don’t be an angry minnow swimming against the tide
  • Banks purge high street branches as balance sheets bulge
  • Mud, mud and more mud as Splendour finally gets under way
  • Splendour in the pub: Bernard Fanning, Baker Boy play pop-up gigs after events cancelled
  • Louis Tomlinson’s Sydney show proof that good things come to those who wait
  • ‘PTSD for 30 years’: ABC’s Eric Campbell on the highs and lows of the best job in the world
  • Neighbours episode 8903: We go on set as the final episode is filmed
  • Rendez-vous causes ripples as swim stars lap up the love
  • What the bald eagle and a tiny bat can tell us about Australia’s broken system for protecting nature
  • Bristlebirds carefully migrate to the Prom by the car-full
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 23 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Cuba to hold referendum on same-sex marriage
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: U.S. denounces ‘outrageous’ attack on Odessa sea port a day after grain deal
  • Russian missiles strike Odessa port day after grain deal, Ukraine says
  • Inside the long, fraught quest to recapture Mexican drug lord Caro Quintero
  • Secret Australian marijuana facility exposes location after turning sky pink
  • After ousting leader, Sri Lanka still staggered by one-two economic punch
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Grain deal signed in Turkey; U.S. to send Ukraine more artillery

  • Uwe Seeler, German soccer champ, dies at 85
  • Russia and Ukraine agree to release blockaded grain exports
  • What to know about Canada’s residential schools and the unmarked graves found nearby
  • Man is killed when sinkhole opens under swimming pool at Israel home
  • U.N. court rejects Myanmar’s opposition to Rohingya genocide case
  • 5 countries hit hard by the grain crisis in Ukraine
  • Prince George grins on beach in new photo released to mark 9th birthday
  • Stolen artifacts, some from 400s B.C., returned to Italy from New York
  • Flash floods kill at least 21 people in southern Iran
  • Saudi police arrest man who helped Israeli sneak into Mecca
  • Chinese court executes man who set ex-wife on fire
  • Pope's Indigenous tour signals a rethink of mission legacy

  • Trump’s choices escalated tensions and set U.S. on path to Jan. 6, panel finds
  • Hearings test Trump’s clout and GOP’s wish to ‘forget about Jan. 6’
  • Man accused of posing as federal law enforcement official signals guilty plea
  • Post Politics Now: Trump, Pence make dueling appearances in Arizona, as both eye 2024
  • What we know — and don’t know — about what Trump did on Jan. 6
  • Post Politics Now: ‘Folks, I’m doing great,’ Biden says after positive coronavirus test
  • Michelle Childs is confirmed to seat on D.C. Circuit
  • WWE Chairman Vince McMahon retires amid sexual misconduct probe
  • Twitter posts surprise drop in revenue amid battle with Elon Musk
  • In Detroit, the chip shortage has left the city eerily short of cars
  • Big Tech is bracing for a possible recession, spooking other industries
  • Google fired engineer who said its AI was sentient
  • Conservatives Shouldn’t Write Off ‘Trussonomics’ Just Yet
  • Stuck In Air Travel Hell? Blame the Long Shadow of Covid
  • Why Russia’s War in Ukraine Means a Hungrier World
  • China wants to ‘reduce misunderstanding’ with the U.S. It could start by talking.
  • The Secret Service texting scandal demands answers
  • In the skies over Belarus, a false bomb report signaled danger on the ground
  • The 988 hotline could be transformational — if done right
  • 5 things we learned in the Jan. 6 committee’s stunning hearing
  • Is 18 minutes enough time for a subject to comment?
  • Cartoons by Clay Jones, Drew Sheneman and Mike Smith
  • Sri Lanka’s former president must be investigated for war crimes
  • Girl Scouts should not sell a Maryland forest to developers
  • Youngkin’s appointments raise questions about his ‘moderate’ label
  • To stop gun violence, we need to fight systemic injustice
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.content:

entry.title: Guardian: 23 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Russian missiles hit Odesa port hours after grain export deal agreed – live

  • Grain deal clears one of many hurdles to get Ukraine’s crops to market

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 23 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 23 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-23 10:50AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Science whizz-kid, tennis champ among winners of Hong Kong’s Student of the Year Awards
  • Hong Kong Book Fair 2022: Meta creative head joins advertising veterans to launch motivational title for young residents
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong ‘to boost jab uptake for unvaccinated seniors, seek data on inoculating toddlers’ as city logs 4,265 new cases

  • Job market picks up for Hong Kong graduates, with high demand for engineering, technology sub-degree holders
  • Hong Kong needs its own student exchange scheme to help young people develop a pan-Asian identity

  • Hong Kong Observatory logs second hottest July day on record at 35.6 degrees Celsius and says heat to continue

  • China follows US on electronic warfare with J-15 and J-10 variants

  • China plans to turn the moon into an outpost for defending the Earth from asteroids, say scientists
  • China’s navy has quietly commissioned a new submarine for its eastern fleet
  • Chinese team tracks beavers with AI, making Xinjiang wildlife work easier
  • Hong Kong scientists work on replicating lunar soil to help China build on the moon
  • China says progress is being made in India border talks, but New Delhi warns of stalemate
  • China’s gaming industry insiders praise new policy document but remain cautious on licensing prospects
  • China on guard as European Central Bank, US Fed interest rate hikes threaten greater spillover effects
  • Chinese video-sharing platform Bilibili cracks down on malicious content, banned behaviour spread by virtual live-streamers
  • Sri Lanka urged by rights groups to stop use of force after armed troops attack protesters
  • Pakistan’s Imran Khan may yet unseat Shehbaz Sharif as prime minister. But not without the military’s help
  • Japan may not allow Russia’s Putin to attend Shinzo Abe’s state funeral: report
  • How a plastics ban turned Siquijor into a zero-waste island in the Philippines
  • What is foot and mouth disease and will it hurt Australian beef supply?
  • Why South Korea’s Quad overtures are falling on deaf ears
  • Are YouTuber’s videos of North Korean parties ‘daily life’ or ‘propaganda’?
  • Corrupt officials keep forcing China to raise the bar for death penalties as it ‘scrapes poison off the bone’
  • Why do Singapore and Malaysia have a more favourable view of China than the US?
  • Myanmar genocide case can proceed, top UN court rules as it throws out all junta’s objections
  • Thai police search for missing monkeypox patient in Phuket
  • Indonesian law targeting ‘disturbing’ online posts harms free speech, data privacy: critics
  • US reports first monkeypox cases in children
  • Google fires engineer who claimed LaMDA AI chatbot was sentient
  • Will Donald Trump be prosecuted over US Capitol attack?
  • Why we cannot let journalism die: the Ukraine war highlights the importance of traditional news in a world dominated by social media influencers
  • Monkeypox virus could become entrenched as new STD in the US, experts say
  • UK leadership candidate Liz Truss pledges to ditch all EU laws by 2023
  • Hundreds march as Warsaw marks 80th anniversary of Jewish ghetto liquidation
  • Polar bear rescued in Russia after getting tongue stuck in milk can
  • Former Trump adviser Steve Bannon convicted of contempt of US Congress
  • France’s Macron hosts close ally Egypt’s al-Sisi in Paris to discuss security and defence ties
  • Joe Biden’s Covid-19 symptoms have improved, White House physician says
  • Poland to buy tanks, howitzers and fighter planes from South Korea
  • UK blames France as travellers face hours-long port delays
  • US will hold Russia accountable for implementing UN-brokered deal to resume Ukraine’s Black Sea grain exports
  • ‘My children are gone’, cries father as Russian air strike on Syria kills at least 7
  • Iran’s nukes ‘galloping ahead’– IAEA chief warns, unable to monitor programme
  • Ukraine war: whether Western sanctions on Russia are working is only part of the question
  • Evergrande crisis: property developer’s CEO and financial chief forced out amid loan inquiry
  • Shanghai bets on infrastructure, fixed-asset investment to claw back economic losses from two-month citywide lockdown
  • From 30kg overweight and depressed to Ironman competitor – how a Singapore expat addicted to alcohol and junk food turned his life around
  • How to pack light and avoid lost or delayed baggage this summer – curate your wardrobe and fit everything in a carry-on bag
  • Serving Chinese food as an entrée to East Asian culture, cafe owner from Hong Kong hopes to get people of Birmingham, UK, talking
  • 6 good reasons to visit China after travel restrictions are lifted: government announces new highest-ranking tourist destinations
  • Single-origin coffee from Yunnan in China, known for Pu-erh tea, finds fans in the West and at home
  • Comedian and actress Margaret Cho on racism, Asian erasure from US history and inspiring a new generation of Asian-American entertainers
  • Piriformis syndrome, a tight buttock, can cause lower back pain in runners, here’s the best prevention techniques
  • Decaying palaces turned into boutique hotels in India – think chandeliers, gazebos, lots of mahogany and Greco-Roman columns
  • Meet Frédéric Arnault, the Tag Heuer CEO vying to run LVMH: Bernard Arnault’s third son bagged Ryan Gosling as brand ambassador, plays tennis with Roger Federer and launched an NFT-friendly watch
  • Who is Ronan Farrow, Woody Allen and Mia Farrow’s son – and respected journalist? His new documentary Endangered is now on HBO Max and he once won a Pulitzer prize for exposing Harvey Weinstein
  • Supercar Blondie tours Dubai mansion with underwater garage: the YouTuber showed off the US$40 million property and its car collection of LaFerraris, a Ford GT and McLaren Senna worth US$24 million
  • 12 richest Baywatch stars – the OG’s net worths, ranked: from iconic cast members Pamela Anderson, David Hasselhoff and Carmen Electra, to Aquaman Jason Momoa, RHOBH’s Lisa Rinna, and Sofía Vergara
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
